items.label: Self

primary_mode.label: Inner positioning
primary_mode.text: When situations shift, your initial instinct is likely to find your footing and clarify your specific role before fully moving forward.

aspects.label: Source
aspects.attention: You seem to pay close attention to the roots of an issue, preferring to understand the real reasons behind decisions and figuring out which responsibilities actually belong to you.
aspects.strength: This approach can give your choices a grounded quality.
aspects.friction: It can feel frustrating when your efforts are misread, or when something you worked hard to build is written off as luck or circumstance.

aspects.label: Direction
aspects.attention: Because the {label} lens tracks where things are going, a situation usually becomes clearer to you once you can see what new paths are opening up.
aspects.strength: This perspective can help you remain steady during transitions that leave others disoriented.
aspects.friction: You might find the ambiguous in-between phases difficult to navigate when the next chapter has not quite formed yet.

aspects.label: Capacity
aspects.attention: You also appear drawn to potential, asking how you need to grow to meet upcoming challenges.
aspects.strength: This mindset can foster patience for long-term development.
aspects.friction: It can be draining when that growth is blocked and your potential has nowhere to go.

items.core_question: What is taking shape inside you, and what will it take to make that real?

items.label: Social

primary_mode.label: Field reading
primary_mode.text: You may notice how roles, responsibilities, and pressures are distributed in a given situation.

aspects.label: Arrangement
aspects.attention: When things change, you likely find yourself tracking how the shift impacts the bigger picture.
aspects.strength: It may be relatively easy for you to spot who is carrying the heaviest load, or to sense unspoken tension that everyone feels but no one has named.
aspects.friction: It can be frustrating when a system only functions because someone is taking on more than their fair share, or when unfair imbalances become the accepted norm.

aspects.label: Reception
aspects.attention: Because the {label} lens is tuned into the shared environment, you likely pay close attention to how new or disruptive elements are received.
aspects.strength: This awareness can make you thoughtful about integrating unfamiliar changes.
aspects.friction: It can be difficult to watch something get rejected simply because a group does not yet know how to accommodate it.

aspects.label: Passage
aspects.attention: You might also feel a pull toward transitions, spotting when a chapter has ended but lacks true closure.
aspects.strength: This can help you notice what remains unresolved after a change.
aspects.friction: It can feel heavy when unresolved issues are left without an answer.

items.core_question: What is happening in the wider environment, and what will it take to keep that space whole?
